title: Pair on_exit with per-test state cleanup without DB writes
prompt: A test needs to clean up an ETS table at test exit. Write `test/my_app/cache_table_test.exs` that: (1) is `async: true`, (2) creates a unique ETS table in setup using `:ets.new(make_ref(), [:public, :named_table])` — use the ref-based name for uniqueness, (3) uses `on_exit(fn -> :ets.delete(table_name) end)` — ETS delete is safe in on_exit because it doesn't touch the sandbox, (4) includes a test that uses the table. Do NOT do any Repo calls in on_exit.
tier_rationale: Requires understanding when on_exit IS safe (non-DB cleanup) vs not.
